questionText: Among the following, the energy of 2s orbital is lowest in :
subject: Chemistry
solution:
Here Z of K = 15, Na = 11, H = 1, Li = 3.

In K, because of more number of protons (high atomic number) the 2s electron experiences a higher effective nuclear charge and is closer to nucleus, thus having less energy

questionText: The size of the iso-electronic species Cl<sup>–</sup>,  Ar and Ca<sup>2+</sup> is affected by :
options: electron-electron interaction in the outer orbitals
Principal quantum number of valence shell
azimuthal quantum number of valence shell
subject: Chemistry
solution:
Cl- Ar Ca+2
Protons 17 18 20
Electrons 18 18 18

Nuclear charge means number of protons present in the nucleus. Here maximum number of protons present in the Ca+2 ion, so the attraction towards those 18 electrons wll be most by the nucleus of the Ca+2 ion. That is why size of the Ca+2 ion wll be least because of highest nuclear charge.
